candidate.title: Market Access Right
candidate.description: Marketing rights that grant entry, participation, or entitlement to use an event, venue, program, or offer.
candidate.activityDistinction: Each member IS A Market Access Right because casino access, event admission, memberships, and passes are all marketed as rights to enter, participate in, or receive controlled access rather than as ordinary goods.

h2.rationale: The is-a test fails because Market Casino and Market Event are not kinds of Market Access Right, while the set does not form a clean single facet distinct from the excluded sellable objects.
h2.weakness: Only Sell Membership and Sell Pass plausibly involve selling access rights; the other proposed members are events or venues being marketed rather than access-right objects.

candidate.concept: All of these denote selling a generic sellable physical object or article of commerce.

judge.name: CanonicalTitleChooser
judge.rationale: Sell Supply can denote selling supplies or provisions, a narrower replenishable-use category with different buyers and processes, while the others are generic articles of commerce.

candidate.concept: All of these denote selling a drink or consumable liquid refreshment.

judge.name: CanonicalTitleChooser
judge.rationale: Sell Beverage specifically denotes selling drinks, while Sell Refreshment can also denote selling light food or snacks, an activity-relevant distinction given siblings like Sell Food and Sell Chip.
